title: Stretch of a Tightrope Wire
body: During a walk on a rope, a tightrope walker creates a tension of $$3.94\times{10}^3$$ N in a wire that is stretched between two supporting poles that are $$15.0$$ $$m$$ apart. The wire has a diameter of $$0.50$$ cm when it is not stretched. When the walker is on the wire in the middle between the poles, the wire makes an angle of $$5.0$$ degrees below the horizontal. Take Young's modulus of steel to be $$2\times{10}^{11}$$ $$\frac{N}{m^2}$$.
